stories.headline: Iran war live: US bombs Sirik, Qeshm, Bandar Abbas over Hormuz attacks
stories.summary: Explosions in Iran's Sirik, Bandar Abbas and Qeshm as US launches attacks after revoking waivers on Iranian oil exports.

stories.headline: US launches new wave of Iran strikes after attacks on tankers
stories.summary: Trump administration also revokes licence allowing Islamic republic’s oil sales, sending crude prices higher

stories.headline: US Strikes Iran and Blocks Oil Sales
stories.summary: The US launched airstrikes in Iran and revoked a waiver that allowed it to sell oil globally, further imperiling a peace agreement after a series of attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's Nick Wadhams breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: These Maine Democrats Might Replace Graham Platner in the Senate Race
stories.summary: Mr. Platner has not yet withdrawn from the race after a rape allegation, but Democrats in the state and nationally are already exploring potential alternatives.

stories.headline: Marine Le Pen to run for French Presidency despite criminal conviction
stories.summary: Marine Le Pen, the far-right French politician, announced Tuesday she is running for president next year.

stories.summary: Villagers whose families have been stranded told the BBC they are struggling to get food and help.

stories.headline: Australian house prices are falling but are still almost double what they were a decade ago
stories.summary: The average dwelling price has risen to more than 17 years of a typical household’s disposable incomeWant to get more charts in your inbox every fortnight? Sign up for The Crunch hereHouse prices have dropped across several Australian capital cities, with major banks predicting falls of 2% to 3% by the end of the year.But after a decade of accelerating house prices, this wouldn’t make a significant dent in housing affordability. Continue reading.

stories.headline: US launches strikes on Iran after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US says the strikes are intended to impose "heavy costs", while Iran warns it will "take decisive measures" in response.

stories.headline: US Strikes Iran and Blocks Oil Sales in New Threats to Ceasefire
stories.summary: The US launched fresh airstrikes in Iran and revoked a waiver that allowed it to sell oil globally, further imperiling a peace agreement after a series of attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: U.S. Strikes Iran and Reimposes Sanctions After Tanker Attacks
stories.summary: The military operation came hours after the U.S. Treasury revoked a waiver allowing global sales of Iranian oil.

stories.headline: U.S. Revokes Iran Oil Sanctions Waiver After Attack on Tankers
stories.summary: A 60-day exemption allowing the sale of Iranian oil was cut short after three tankers were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Le Pen says she will appeal against conviction and run for French presidency – Europe live as it happened
stories.summary: Far-right leader gives TV interview saying that she will appeal against part of today’s ruling which cast doubt on a presidential run The opening speeches are now under way in Ankara, and you can watch them below.This is the Day 1 industry event, not the leaders’ summit, mind you. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Why is Marine Le Pen running for French presidency after court confirmed conviction?
stories.summary: A look at the case, the appeal court decision and how it could affect the race to succeed Emmanuel MacronMarine Le Pen has said she will be a candidate in France’s presidential race next year, after a court ruling on her appeal against a conviction for embezzlement of public funds allowed her to run.However, the path ahead is far from straightforward. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Zelenskyy renews call for Ukraine's NATO membership citing military might ahead of summit
stories.summary: The Ukrainian President has renewed his push to join NATO, insisting his country has already proved itself a reliable partner and that its membership in the alliance would be “entirely natural”.

stories.headline: China test-fires long-range missile into Pacific, rattling U.S. allies
stories.summary: The unprecedented test of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile highlights Beijing’s growing force in the region.

stories.headline: US says strikes launched as explosions heard in southern Iran
stories.summary: US strikes in Iran come a day after Qatari, Saudi tankers hit in attacks in Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Marine Le Pen found guilty, but court clears way for presidential run if she wears tag
stories.summary: The National Rally leader now has to decide whether she will run with an electronic tag and will speak on TV later.

stories.headline: US revokes waiver allowing Iranian oil sales after tanker strikes in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Qatari and Saudi ships among targets near Oman coast

stories.headline: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she will run for president
stories.summary: Politician sets up unprecedented election campaign next year after judges uphold her conviction for embezzlement

stories.headline: Oil Rises, Asian Stocks Steady as US Strikes Iran: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Oil prices rose and Asian stocks were little changed as investors assessed the implications of renewed geopolitical tensions for energy supplies and risk assets.

stories.headline: Oil Surges as US Strikes Targets in Iran Following Ship Attacks
stories.summary: Oil jumped as the US launched a wave of strikes against Iran in retaliation for a spate of attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: U.S. launches ‘powerful strikes’ against Iran, citing attacks on commercial ships
stories.summary: The latest violence followed accusations by Qatar that Iran attacked a tanker ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Marine Le Pen to run for presidency and appeal against conviction in France’s highest court
stories.summary: French far-right leader announces decision after court orders her to wear ankle tag over embezzlement The French far-right leader Marine Le Pen has announced she will run for the presidency in 2027 and will lodge an appeal to France’s highest court over her sentence to wear an electronic ankle tag for the embezzlement of European parliament funds.“Tonight, I am a candidate in the presidential election,” Le Pen, 57, told TF1 television on Tuesday

stories.headline: France’s Marine Le Pen must wear ankle tag after appeal ruling
stories.summary: A French appeals court upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for misusing European Parliament funds.

stories.headline: French far-right leader Le Pen says will run in 2027 presidential election
stories.summary: Her announcement followed a court ruling that reduced the length of time she was banned from holding public office.

stories.headline: Trump lashes out at NATO as first day of summit wraps in Turkiye
stories.summary: Leaders have gathered in Ankara for a two-day summit slated to cover defence spending and the Russia-Ukraine war.

stories.headline: Marine Le Pen vows to run in France’s 2027 presidential election despite fraud conviction
stories.summary: The leader of the National Rally announced on French TV that she would remain a candidate for the 2027 presidential election despite a court finding her guilty of embezzling EU funds.

stories.headline: Russian missiles strike Ukraine’s capital, Kyiv, for third time in a week
stories.summary: The attacks have triggered fires in two districts of Kyiv, according to the city's mayor.

stories.headline: Marine Le Pen's moment of truth: Why Tuesday court verdict matters for France
stories.summary: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.

stories.headline: Fatal Typhoon Maysak floods burst dam wall in China
stories.summary: At least four have been killed by flooding due to Maysak, while another 62,000 have been evacuated across the southern Guangxi province.

stories.headline: Bernie Sanders Calls on Graham Platner to Drop Senate Bid After Rape Allegation
stories.summary: Mr. Sanders was one of the last remaining prominent allies of Graham Platner, the Maine Senate Democratic nominee, to call for him to drop out after a rape allegation.

stories.headline: Democratic factions vie to pick Platner replacement before he leaves Maine race
stories.summary: Potential candidates and their backers moved quickly to gain an edge in the contest over who would replace Graham Platner if he exits the key Senate race.

stories.headline: 6 potential replacements for Graham Platner if he drops out of Senate race
stories.summary: The Maine Democrat has faced an escalating string of scandals as he competes for a critical Senate seat that could determine control of the chamber.

stories.headline: Top Democrats pull Graham Platner endorsements, call on him to end Maine Senate campaign
stories.summary: The Democrat, who has withstood other scandals since launching his challenge to Sen. Susan Collins (R), is accused of sexual assault by a woman he previously dated.

stories.headline: Bomb explosions injure 18 in Damascus during Macron's visit
stories.summary: As reports of the blasts came in, state television said the Syrian president had welcomed Macron at the presidential palace.

stories.headline: Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Inks $2 Billion Partnership With Morrison
stories.summary: Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank will take a 15% stake in New Zealand-based infrastructure investor Morrison, as part of a new strategic partnership to provide its Japanese customers access to fresh investment opportunities.

stories.headline: US Judge Rejects DOJ Subpoena Over 2020 Georgia Election
stories.summary: A federal judge rejected an effort by the US Justice Department to obtain information on election workers in Fulton County, Georgia, related to the 2020 vote.

stories.headline: What will define Elon Musk’s legacy? Doge cuts to USAID Ebola programs

SpaceX stock dropped precipitously after its initial public offering, and Tesla faces a wave of lawsuits. stories.headline: India's landmark Ram temple overhauls board after alleged theft of donations
stories.summary: Questions have been raised over alleged theft of cash, valuable jewellery, gold and silver offered by devotees.

stories.headline: Why some African nations are turning down Trump aid money
stories.summary: The Trump administration sees the aid as more transactional - but some say it is not a fair trade.

stories.headline: Meta releases first image model since Zuckerberg’s AI overhaul
stories.summary: Muse Spark Image will be integrated into tech giant’s AI chatbot and its Instagram photo app

stories.headline: IMF names Silvana Tenreyro as its next chief economist
stories.summary: Former Bank of England policymaker will be second woman to take on the role

stories.headline: S&P Dow Jones Signals Possible Frontier-Market Cut for Indonesia
stories.summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices has listed Indonesia as a country for potential downgrade to frontier-market, saying it may first consider implementing special treatment for its securities if circumstances worsen.

stories.headline: Canada Offers Teck C$400 Million for Strategic Metals Growth
stories.summary: The Canadian government will contribute as much as C$400 million ($282 million) to Teck Resources Ltd.’s plans to boost output at a key metals refinery, creating a deal that could ensure the country receives a portion of future germanium, antimony and gallium supply.

stories.headline: GM-Backed Momenta Set for Hong Kong Debut After $752 Million IPO
stories.summary: Momenta Global Ltd., a Chinese autonomous-driving firm, is set to begin trading in Hong Kong on Wednesday after raising HK$5.9 billion ($752 million) in an initial public offering, testing market appetite for loss-making firms that offer promising technologies.

stories.headline: Temasek-Backed Foundation Healthcare Set for Singapore Debut
stories.summary: Foundation Healthcare Holdings Ltd is set to begin trading Wednesday in Singapore after an initial public offering that was the largest of the year so far in the city state outside real estate investment trusts.

stories.headline: Amazon’s New Bonds Get Cooler Reception as AI Debt Floods Market
stories.summary: When Amazon.com Inc. sold its biggest ever bond earlier this year, it was inundated with investor orders amid hype about the artificial intelligence boom. This time around there’s less fanfare.

stories.headline: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda.Catnip, or Nepeta cataria, is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria – nepetalactone – also has insect-repelling properties but this

stories.headline: SpaceX shares slide as it joins the tech-heavy Nasdaq-100
stories.summary: Musk-led company joined the index on Tuesday, less than a month after its stock market debut on June 12.

stories.headline: Nairobi man shares home with rescued birds
stories.summary: Nairobi's 'Birdman' Rodgers Magutha rescues injured birds and lives with them, while defending his unusual bond with wildlife and conservation work.

stories.headline: Morocco multiplies its olive oil sales in Spain by 100: is it taking over the market?
stories.summary: Imports of Moroccan olive oil have surged 9,979% in a year, according to DataComex. The figure is real but starts from just 103 tonnes and its share in Spain remains small.

stories.headline: EU still can't prove €43 billion in home renovation funds actually saved energy
stories.summary: The EU accelerated home renovations after the pandemic, but according to its own auditors, it still lacks convincing evidence that the program delivered the energy savings and value for taxpayers that justified its massive investment.

stories.headline: ECB tells Europe's biggest banks to prepare for AI-powered cyber threats
stories.summary: The European Central Bank has given the eurozone's largest lenders until the end of October to explain how they will strengthen their cyber defences against increasingly capable AI systems.

stories.headline: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 23 killed in Kyiv region
stories.summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

stories.headline: Oil Jumps After Ship Strikes Spur US to Revoke Iran Sales Waiver
stories.summary: Oil climbed almost 6% after attacks on Persian Gulf shipping prompted the US to revoke a waiver that had relaxed restrictions on Iranian crude sales.

stories.headline: China tests missile in the Pacific hours after Australia-Fiji alliance signed
stories.summary: The test launch came hours after Australia signed a new defence pact with its Pacific island neighbour Fiji.

stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.

stories.headline: Headteacher among four killed in deadliest Israeli attack on Lebanon since latest ceasefire
stories.summary: Three women and one man were travelling in a car after visiting their family home, Lebanese state media reported.

stories.headline: After America’s 250th, Trump will test how far he can push NATO allies
stories.summary: As NATO leaders gather in Turkey, they are preparing for another summit shaped as much by managing President Donald Trump as by the alliance's security agenda.

stories.summary: Tropical storm causes extreme flooding in south of the country with heavy rainfall expected in coming daysA tropical storm has killed two people, caused dam breaches and forced tens of thousands to evacuate in southern China.Typhoon Maysak killed two people in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi province. stories.headline: Gold Slumps as Traders Weigh Fed Rate Path After Iran Oil Waiver
stories.summary: Gold slumped after the US Treasury Department revoked a waiver allowing the sale of Iranian oil, stoking concern that elevated energy prices will prompt the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

stories.headline: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
stories.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised for remarks he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.

stories.headline: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
stories.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
stories.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

You can liter

stories.headline: Moment of destiny for France's Le Pen in verdict to decide her future in presidential race
stories.summary: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen is appealing against a verdict which found her guilty of misusing EU funds.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: 'Two weeks after her death I got a call': Gaza patients face agonising delays for evacuation
stories.summary: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.headline: The US deported them to Venezuela - hours later earthquakes struck
stories.summary: They landed on Flight 164 just before the twin quakes. Family members are desperately searching for them.

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off
stories.summary: The US has confirmed it will not extend the agreement for another 16 years, but stopped short of more dramatic action.

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament
stories.summary: Fifteen women are appointed, after only six female candidates were selected by regional electoral colleges last October.

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Will Grant reports from Caraballeda, La Guaira, where locals are still desperately searching for family members in the rubble.
